_meta.description: SYX Design System — Executable rule contract. Used by scripts/syx-validate.js to run automated checks.

severities.error: Must be fixed before release. Breaks token contract or introduces technical debt.
severities.warning: Should be fixed. Indicates misalignment between source and runtime.
severities.info: Informational. Catalogued but no blocking action required.

rules.description: No --primitive-* tokens in atoms/, molecules/, organisms/ or pages/ SCSS files
rules.rationale: Primitive tokens are raw values. Components must always go through --semantic-* or --component-* to enable theming.
SYX uses @layer for cascade management. !important is never needed and indicates a layer assignment error.
The @include transition() mixin automatically adds prefers-reduced-motion guard. Raw transition: skips this.

rules.description: No raw `position: absolute`, `position: fixed`, or `position: sticky` — use SYX positioning mixins
rules.rationale: Positioning mixins are null-safe and consistent. Raw position declarations bypass the null-safe pattern.

rules.description: Component tokens defined in SCSS should be registered in tokens.json
rules.rationale: tokens.json is the source-of-truth documentation. Undocumented tokens are invisible to AI tooling.

rules.description: Tokens documented in tokens.json should appear in the compiled CSS runtime
rules.rationale: A token that exists in documentation but not in CSS output is a phantom token. AI suggestions using it will fail at runtime.

rules.description: CSS custom properties without an official SYX prefix (--primitive-*, --semantic-*, --component-*, --theme-*, --icon-*, --layout-*, --reset-*) must be catalogued
rules.rationale: Unprefixed variables are invisible to the AI contract layer and may be legacy, local, or from an external source.

rules.description: Tokens in the registry should have at least one usage in SCSS files
rules.rationale: Tokens that are defined but never used are candidates for removal. Dead tokens add noise to AI context.

knownLegacyOrigins.note: If a legacy var is identified, investigate its origin before migrating. Possible sources: historical SYX files, external CSS resets, or theme builder artifacts.
